reasoning: The 30-year constant maturity US treasury yield behaves approximately like a random walk over short horizons, with a slight possibility of ties due to quoting increments, making the probability of a strict increase slightly below 0.5. Further, expectations of a secular decline in interest rates or a downward drift depending on the macroeconomic context might put slight downward pressure on the yield. In the absence of real-time search capabilities, I rely on the random walk assumption augmented with the historical base rate (0.422). I adopt probabilities slightly below 50% for shorter horizons (0.47 for 1 week, 0.48 for 1 month) to account forties and drift, reverting precisely to 0.50 for the 3-month horizon to reflect max uncertainty.
